auto_merge.commit_message: Substitutes it with plain `dict` in Py<3.15.
auto_merge.commit_title: Implement support and C-API declarations for PEP-814 frozendict (GH-7545)

repo.description: The most widely used Python to C compiler
repo.name: cython

body: Substitutes it with plain `dict` in Py<3.15.

comment_data.body: I'm not sure we need to provide the legacy Python2-era interface for a newly added type.

comment_data.body:
I think for the Limited API the right thing to do is to try to get it from the `types` module or `PyEval_GetBuiltins` (as in `CachedMethodType` or `Coro_CheckExact`) and use that if available.

Since even for Limited API<3.15 there's some chance that the type is actually available at runtime.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -808,3 +808,41 @@ static {{out_type}} __Pyx_PyMemoryView_Get_{{name}}(PyObject *obj) {
 #define __Pyx_PySlice_Stop(o) __Pyx_NewRef(((PySliceObject*)o)->stop)
 #define __Pyx_PySlice_Step(o) __Pyx_NewRef(((PySliceObject*)o)->step)
 #endif
+
+
+////////////// PyFrozenDict.proto /////////////////////////

+#if (defined(PyFrozenDict_Check) && defined(PyAnyDict_Check) && defined(PyFrozenDict_New)) || \
+  (0 && __PYX_LIMITED_VERSION_HEX >= 0x030f0000) || (!CYTHON_COMPILING_IN_LIMITED_API && PY_VERSION_HEX >= 0x030f00a6)
+// PyFrozenDict is not currently in the Limited API.
+#define __Pyx_PyFrozenDict_Type  PyFrozenDict_Type
+#define __Pyx_PyFrozenDict_New(it)  PyFrozenDict_New(it)
+#define __Pyx_PyFrozenDict_NewEmpty()  PyFrozenDict_New(NULL)
+#define __Pyx_PyFrozenDict_Check(obj)  PyFrozenDict_Check(obj)
+#define __Pyx_PyFrozenDict_CheckExact(obj)  PyFrozenDict_CheckExact(obj)
+#define __Pyx_PyAnyDict_Check(obj)  PyAnyDict_Check(obj)
+#define __Pyx_PyAnyDict_CheckExact(obj)  PyAnyDict_CheckExact(obj)
+#else
+// It is questionable to replace PyFrozenDict with PyDict in the Limited API of Py3.15,

comment_data.body:
See https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/145542

It's probably worth holding off on this PR until the Python interface has settled a bit

                                     BuiltinMethod("copy",   "T",   "T", "PyDict_Copy")]),

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
    return PyObject_CallFunctionObjArgs(CGLOBAL(__Pyx_PyFrozenDictType), it, NULL);
```

Arguably you could do the more optimized fallback for the case where `dict` is substituting. I'm not convinced it's worth it here, but it's easy to do.

Admittedly this is only currently used in ImportExport where it doesn't really matter, and the code generation in the `py315_really_has_frozendict` test just goes through `__Pyx_PyObject_FastCall((PyObject*)(__Pyx_PyFrozenDict_TypePtr), ...`

Thinking about it more, the one usage of this function so far is in a context where it's probably better to return whichever type is quickest to construct rather than preferring `frozendict`.

If that's the case I wonder if the naming should be something like `__Pyx_PyFrozenDictOrDict_New` to make it clearer what it does (and then keep your current version which prefers `dict` in the limited API).

> the one usage of this function so far is in a context where it's probably better to return whichever type is quickest to construct

Good point. I'll change the usage to get a frozendict only when it's easily available.

> Admittedly this is only currently used in ImportExport

I guess I was anticipating a backport for the (yet to be added) C-API declarations for `Include/cpython/frozendict.pxd`. But we can probably start simple (as you suggest above) and then see if that's enough.

```suggestion
/////////////// PyFrozenDict.cleanup ////////////////
```

? Don't think this'll get used otherwise

commit.message:
Implement support for PEP-814 frozendict.
Substitutes it with 'dict' in Py<3.15.
Remove legacy Py2 methods from the optimised frozendict methods and add .copy().
Add missing "builtins that construct their instance".
Add frozendict methods to list of known return types.
Use the correct unbound methods for frozendict, not the one from 'dict'.
Fix test.
Try to reuse the new dict in the fallback code.
Remove non-existant "optimised" method.
Remove builtin method optimisation that no longer applies to recent Py3.15 alpha releases.
Merge branch 'master' into add_frozendict
Fix typo in function name.
Implement Limited API fallback.
Avoid dict creation overhead in importing code only if we actually can avoid the overhead.
Avoid unnecessary overhead for the frozendict -> dict fallback.
Add C-API declarations for PyFrozenDict.
Simplify the "__Pyx_PyFrozenDict_New()" implementation in the Limited API.
Backport frozendict C-API to Py<3.15 using 'dict'.
Add frozendict C-API test.
Fix function prototype.
Make sure we include the 'frozendict' utility code whenever 'cpython.frozendict' is cimported.
Fix version guard.
Fix version guard.
Remove useless and redundant comment.
Add tests for optimised "__contains__" method calls.
Fix accidental renaming leftover.

title: Implement support for PEP-814 frozendict
